name: Kurgan Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the silver of the kurgans (burial mounds) and the sky
the steppe and forests of the Trans-Urals; field on which the kurgans stand

description: Three horizontal stripes of equal height - white, emerald green, white - with two overlapping silver kurgan-shaped mounds centered on the green stripe.
history: Adopted on 18 July 2001. Designed by Dmitry Ivanov. The kurgans are a canting reference to the oblast's name (kurgan means burial mound).

features.symbolism: two overlapping silver kurgans (ancient burial mounds), a canting reference to the oblast's name; their silhouettes are separated by an emerald contour.
